UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JOANN MAHER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINTIS SUSTAINED WITH 10-DAY LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF SHALL ALLEGE ALL ELEMENTS OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION IN LIGHT OF JUDICIALLY NOTICEABLE DOCUMENTS AND CIVIL CODE 1066, 1105, 1636 AND 1639. REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS A, B AND C; DENIED AS TO EXHIBITS D AND E. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION PLAINTIFF SHALL SPECIFY WHICH INSTRUMENT SHE SEEKS TO CANCEL. SEE FAC 42 VERSUS OPPOSITION 6:22-23. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION PLAINTIFF SHALL RE-ALLEGE IT AS A REMEDY. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PARTITION IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY AND ITS COUNSEL IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.6C IN FUTURE.. JUDGE: HARRY DORFMAN, CLERK: MARS GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.

Defendant Joann Maher'S Motion For Order To Transfer Action For Coordination Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sec 403
Off Calendar Oct-22-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Russell Stanaland, Esq. of Lerner Veit & Stanaland LLP, (415-781-4000), for Joann M. Maher, Defendant and moving party.
Veronica H. Garcia, Esq. of Goins & Associates, (510-663-3700), for Jinetta Scott, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Joann Maher'S Motion For Order To Transfer Action For Coordination Under Code Of Civil Procedure Sec 403 is GRANTED. Cases CGC-13-528581 (San Francisco), INC-1300844 (Riverside) and 26-61334 (Napa) are transferred for coordination to San Francisco County Superior Court. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Russell Stanaland, Esq., of Lerner, Veit & Stanaland, LLP. (415-781-4000) for Joann Maher, Defendant and Moving Party.
No Appearance by Plaintiff and opposing party.

Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT JOANN MAHER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained WITH 10-Day LEAVE TO AMEND. As to the first and second causes of action, Plaintiff shall allege all elements of each cause of action in light of judicially noticeable documents and Civil Code 1066, 1105, 1636 and 1639. Request for Judicial Notice is Granted as to Defendant's Exhibits A, B and C; DENIED as to Exhibits D and E. As to the third cause of action Plaintiff shall specify which instrument she seeks to cancel. See FAC 42 versus Opposition 6:22-23. As to the fifth cause of action Plaintiff shall re-allege it as a remedy. Plaintiff's request for leave to allege a cause of action for partition is GRANTED. Moving party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. Judge: Harry Dorfman, Clerk: Mars Goodman, not Reported. (D501)
